title: Safety brakelight system
abstract: This disclosure provides a three-color coded brake lights system, analogous to those found in common traffic lights. In one embodiment, a green light may be at the center, yellow or amber lights on both sides of the green light, and red lights adjacent to the yellow or amber lights. The system may operate analogous to a traffic light to signal the tailing driver on the real-time status and or behavior of a lead car, and also significantly mitigate the severity of rear-end and other collisions with the application of a three color-coded system being followed by global motorists for safe driving.
claims: Claims We Claim: 1 . A brake light system for a vehicle comprising: a brake pedal comprising a pressure sensor configured to output a signal corresponding to an amount of pressure on the brake pedal; a microprocessor configured to read the signal from the pressure sensor; brake lights comprising one or more green lights, one or more yellow lights, and one or more red lights; wherein when the brake pedal has about 0% pressure on it, only the one or more green light(s) are on; when the brake pedal has about 1 % to about 90% pressure on it, only the one or more yellow light(s) are on, when the brake pedal has about 90% to about 100% pressure on it, only the one or more red light(s) are on; wherein the pressure ranges from 0% to 100% such that 100% pressure completely stops the vehicleâs wheels and 0% pressure represents no pressure being applied to the brake pedal. 2. The brake light system for a vehicle of claim 1 , wherein the one or more green light(s) are positioned with one or more yellow lights adjacent to and on both sides of the green light(s), and the one or more red lights are positioned adjacent to and on both sides of the one or more yellow lights. 3. The brake light system for a vehicle of claim 2, wherein the one or more green lights, the one or more yellow lights, and the one or more red lights are positioned substantially in a horizontal plane. 4. The brake light system for a vehicle of claim 2, wherein the one or more green lights, the one or more yellow lights, and the one or more red lights are positioned substantially in a vertical plane. 5. The brake light system for a vehicle of claim 1 , wherein the brakes are hydraulic brakes comprising a hydraulic fluid that transmits a pressure from the brake pedal to a vehicle wheel braking system. 6. The brake light system for a vehicle of claim 5, wherein the pressure sensor is a transducer that measures the pressure of the hydraulic brake fluid. 7. A method of warning a driver of a second motor vehicle driving behind a first motor vehicle comprising: displaying one or more green lights on or near the backside of the first motor vehicle when the first motor vehicle is exerting about 0% pressure on the first motor vehicle brake pedal; displaying one or more yellow lights on or near the backside of the first motor vehicle when the first motor vehicle is exerting about 1 % to about 90% pressure on the first motor vehicle brake pedal; displaying one or more red lights on or near the backside of the first motor vehicle when the first motor vehicle exerts about 90% to about 100% pressure on the first motor vehicle brake pedal, wherein pressure ranges from 0% to 100% such that 100% pressure completely stops the vehicleâs wheels and 0% represents no pressure being applied to the brake pedal. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the one or more green light(s) are positioned with one or more yellow lights adjacent to and on both sides of the green light(s), and one or more red lights are positioned adjacent to and on both sides of the one or more yellow lights. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the one or more green lights, one or more yellow lights, and one or more red lights are positioned substantially in a horizontal plane. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the one or more green lights, one or more yellow lights, and one or more red lights are positioned substantially in a vertical plane. 11. The method of claim 7, wherein the brakes are hydraulic brakes comprising a hydraulic fluid that transmits a pressure from a brake pedal to a vehicle wheel braking system.
